title: Pseudomonas jilinensis sp. nov., Isolated from Oil Production Water of Jilin Oilfield in China.
author_string: Wang, Jian-Wei, Cai, Man, Nie, Yong, Hu, Bing, Yang, Ying et al.
abstract: A Gram-stain-negative, rod-shaped bacterial strain JS15-10A1<sup>T</sup> was isolated from oil production water. Its optimum growth was observed at 37 °C, pH 7.0, and 3% (w/v) NaCl. The 16S rRNA gene sequence of strain JS15-10A1<sup>T</sup> showed the highest similarities with Pseudomonas parafulva CB-1<sup>T</sup> (97.6%) and P. fulva IAM 1529<sup>T</sup> (97.5%). In addition, phylogenetic analyses based on multilocus sequence analyses with concatenating 16S rRNA, gyrB, rpoD, and rpoB genes indicated that strain JS15-10A1<sup>T</sup> was a member of genus Pseudomonas but discriminated from other species. Furthermore, whole-genome analyses revealed that average nucleotide identities and in silico DNA-DNA hybridization values of strain JS15-10A1<sup>T</sup> against its closest relatives were all below 76.7% and 21.1%, respectively. The major cellular fatty acids of strain JS15-10A1<sup>T</sup> were summed feature 8 (C<sub>18:1</sub>ω7c/C<sub>18:1</sub>ω6c), C<sub>16:0</sub>, C<sub>12:0</sub>, C<sub>17:0</sub> cyclo, summed feature 3 (C<sub>16:1</sub>ω7c/C<sub>16:1</sub>ω6c), C<sub>12:0</sub> 3-OH, C<sub>10:0</sub> 3-OH, and C<sub>19:0</sub> cyclo ω8c. The predominant quinone was ubiquinone Q-9. The polar lipids were diphosphatidylglycerol, phosphatidylglycerol, phosphatidylethanolamine, an unknown amino-lipid, and two unidentified lipids. The genome DNA G + C content was 60.0 mol%. On the basis of phylogenetic, phenotypic, physiological, and chemotaxonomic analyses, it can be concluded that strain JS15-10A1<sup>T</sup> represents a novel species in genus Pseudomonas, for which the name Pseudomonas jilinensis sp. nov. is proposed. The type strain is JS15-10A1<sup>T</sup> (= CGMCC 1.16072<sup>T</sup> = LMG 30036<sup>T</sup>).
